content: For a MOS capacitor, $$\mathrm{V_{fb}}$$ and $$\mathrm{V_{t}}$$ are the flat-band voltage and the threshold voltage, respectively. The variation of the depletion width ($$\mathrm{W_{dep}}$$) for varying gate voltage ($$\mathrm{V_{g}}$$) is best represented by
explanation:
When $V_g < V_{f b} \Rightarrow W_{d e p}=0$

When $V_{f b} < V_g < V_t \Rightarrow W_{d e p}$ increases

When $V_g > V_t \Rightarrow W_{\text {dep }}$ becomes constant

$\therefore$ Only option (C) satisfies the above conditions

Hence, the correct option is (B).

Transistor geometries in a CMOS inverter have been adjusted to meet the requirement for worst case charge and discharge times for driving a load capacitor C. This design is to be converted to that of a NOR circuit in the same technology, so that its worst case charge and discharge times while driving the same capacitor are similar. The channel lengths of all transistors are to be kept unchanged. Which one of the following statements is correct?

Widths of PMOS transistors should be doubled, while widths of NMOS transistors should
be halved.

Widths of PMOS transistors should be doubled, while widths of NMOS transistors should
not be changed.

Widths of PMOS transistors should be halved, while widths of NMOS transistors should
not be changed.

Widths of PMOS transistors should be unchanged, while widths of NMOS
transistors should be halved.

content: The threshold voltage of an n-channel MOSFET can be increased by

Consider a MOS capacitor made with p-type silicon. It has an oxide thickness of 100 nm, a fixed positive oxide charge of $10^{-8}$ C/cm2 at the oxide-silicon interface, and a metal work function of 4.6 eV. Assume that the relative permittivity of the oxide is 4 and the absolute permittivity of free space is $8.85 × 10^{-14}$ F/cm. If the flatband voltage is 0 V, the work function of the p-type silicon (in eV, rounded off to two decimal places) is ______.
explanation:
Given : MOS capacitor made with p-type silicon

$$ \begin{aligned} & t_{o x}=100 \mathrm{~nm} \\ & Q_{o x}=10^{-8} \mathrm{c} / \mathrm{cm}^2 \\ & \phi_m=4.6 \mathrm{eV} \\ & \varepsilon_{r_{\mathrm{SiO}_2}}=4 \\ & \varepsilon_0=8.85 \times 10^{-14} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m} \\ & V_{F B}=0 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Flat band voltage is given by, }\\ &\begin{aligned} & V_{F B}=\phi_{m s}-\frac{Q_{o x}}{C_{o x}} \\ & V_{F B}=\phi_m-\phi_s-\frac{Q_{o x}}{C_{o x}} \quad\left[\because C_{o x}=\frac{\varepsilon_{o x}}{t_{o x}}\right] \\ & V_{F B}=\phi_m-\phi_s-\frac{Q_{o x}}{\frac{\varepsilon_{o x}}{t_{o x}}} \\ & V_{F B}=\phi_m-\phi_s-\frac{Q_{o x}}{\frac{\varepsilon_{0_{\mathrm{SiO}_2}}}{t_{o x}}} \\ & 0=\phi_m-\phi_s-\frac{Q_{o x}}{\frac{\varepsilon_{0_{\mathrm{SiO}_2}}}{t_{o x}}} \\ & \phi_s=\phi_m-\frac{Q_{o x}}{\frac{\varepsilon_{0_{\mathrm{SiO}_2}}}{t_{o x}}} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \phi_s=4.6-\frac{10^{-8}}{\frac{8.85 \times 10^{-14} \times 4}{100 \times 10^{-9} \times 10^2}} \\ & \phi_s=4.6-0.2834=4.32 \mathrm{eV} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the work function of the p-type silicon is 4.32 eV .

content: In the circuit below, the voltage V$$_{\mathrm{L}}$$ is _____________ V (rounded off to two decimal places).
explanation:
Given circuit can be drawn as shown below

From the above figure,

$$ \begin{aligned} & I_1=\frac{10}{1} \times 1 \mathrm{~mA}=10 \mathrm{~mA} \\\\ & I_2=7 \times 1 \mathrm{~mA}=7 \mathrm{~mA} \\\\ & I_3=\frac{5}{10} \times 10 \mathrm{~mA}=5 \mathrm{~mA} \end{aligned} $$

KCL at node $x \Rightarrow I=I_2-I_3 \Rightarrow I=7 \mathrm{~mA}-5 \mathrm{~mA}=2 \mathrm{~mA}$

$$ \therefore V_L=2 \mathrm{~mA} \times 1 \mathrm{k} \Omega=2 \mathrm{~V} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 2 .

content: The band diagram of $p$-type semiconductor with a bandgap of the 1 eV is shown. Using this semiconductor, a MOS capacitor having $V_{T H}$ of $-0.16 \mathrm{~V}, C_{o x}^{\prime}$ of $100 \mathrm{nF} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ and metal work function of 3.87 eV is fabricated. There is no charge within the oxide. If the voltage across the capacitor is $V_{T H}$, the magnitude of depletion charge per unit area (in $\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ ) is
explanation:
Given : $V_{\text {th }}=-0.16 \mathrm{~V}, \phi_m=3.879 \mathrm{eV}, C_{0 x}=100 \mu \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ and $E_C-E_V=1 \mathrm{eV}$.

The band diagram is shown in below figure,

The threshold voltage is given by,

$$ V_{t h}=\phi_{m s}+2 \phi_{f p}-\frac{Q_d}{C_{o x}}-\frac{Q_{o x}}{C_{o x}} $$

Given that, oxide charges is zero, therefore

$$ \begin{aligned} & V_{t h}=\phi_{m s}+2 \phi_{f p}-\frac{Q_d}{C_{o x}} \\ & -0.16=3.879-(4+0.5+0.3)+2\left(E_i-E_f\right)-\frac{Q_d}{C_{o x}} \\ & \frac{Q_d}{C_{o x}}=3.879+0.16+0.6-4.8=-0.161 \\ & Q_d=-0.161 \times 100 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{cm}^2=-16.1 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{cm}^2 \\ & Q_d=1.61 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{cm}^2 \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (D).
